data.title: AgomAb Therapeutics NV reports FY results
data.description:
• AgomAb Therapeutics NV press release (AGMB): Net loss was €62.5 million for the full year ended December 31, 2025, compared to €46.3 million for the full year ended December 31, 2024.
  • Basic and diluted loss per share was €143.22.
  • Research and development (R&D) expenses were €48.9 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, as compared with €39.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. 
  • Cash, cash equivalents and short-term cash investments totaled €116.5 million as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: Iran's foreign minister said to visit Islamabad tonight
data.description:
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is scheduled to arrive in Islamabad on Friday night for what could be a second round of peace negotiations with the U.S., according to a media report.

A U.S. logistics and security team has already arrived in Pakistan’s capital to prepare for the discussions, Bloomberg reported, citing Pakistani officials familiar with the matter.

The potential resumption of negotiations comes after President Donald Trump canceled plans to send Vice President JD Vance to Islamabad earlier this week, following an indefinite extension of the ceasefire with Iran.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Meta inks agreement with Amazon to use tens of millions of Graviton chips to power AI
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) continues to generate large deals with its in-house chips, with Meta Platforms (META) being the latest.

Under the new agreement, Meta plans to deploy "tens of millions" of AWS's Graviton processors to power its AI as the two companies expand their partnership. Meta will use the Graviton5, a chip featuring 192 cores and a cache five times larger than its predecessor. The greater bandwidth helps agentic AI systems. The chip is built on 3-nanometer technology.

"This isn't just about chips; it's about giving customers the infrastructure foundation, as well as data and inference services, to build AI that understands, anticipates, and scales efficiently to billions of people worldwide," said Nafea Bshara, vice president and distinguished engineer at Amazon. "Meta's expanded partnership, deploying tens of millions of Graviton cores, shows what happens when you combine purpose-built silicon with the full AWS AI stack to power the next generation of agentic AI." 

"As we scale the infrastructure behind Meta's AI ambitions, diversifying our compute sources is a strategic imperative," added Santosh Janardhan, head of infrastructure, Meta.

The deal also exemplifies the importance of CPUs in AI workloads related to agentic tasks, such as real-time reasoning, code generation, search, and orchestrating multi-step tasks.

Financial details of the deal were not made public, but it is expected to last for at least three years.

Earlier this week, Amazon struck a deal with Anthropic to provide the Claude maker with up to 5 GW of capacity powered by AWS's Tranium chips and Graviton cores. Anthropic agreed to spend more than $100B over the next decade as part of the agreement.

These agreements should benefit companies such as Marvell (MRVL), Astera Labs (ALAB), and Credo Technology (CRDO) as they serve as key suppliers for Amazon.

Amazon shares edged up nearly 2% during pre-market trading on Friday. Marvell and Astera Labs were both up 3%. Credo crept up 3.5%.

data.title: ChoiceOne Financial Services GAAP EPS of $0.91, revenue of $42.45M
data.description:
• ChoiceOne Financial Services press release (COFS): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.91.
  • Revenue of $42.45M.

data.title: Alpha expects to report Q1 loss of $11M
data.description:
• Alpha Metallurgical Resources (AMR) said that it expects Q1 net loss of $11M, or $0.86 per diluted share, during its preliminary report on Friday.
  • It sold 3.6M tons of coal during the period generating total revenues of $523.5M. Total adjusted EBITDA for Q1 was $30.0 million.
  • As of March 31, 2026, the company had total liquidity of $476.2 million, including cash and cash equivalents of $317.2 million, short-term investments of $49.6 million, and $184.3 million of unused availability under the asset-based revolving credit facility (ABL).
  • The company plans to release its definitive first quarter financial results on May 8, 2026.
  • AMR -14.76% premarket to $169.56.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MainStreet Bancshares approves new $10M share buyback
data.description:
• MainStreet Bancshares (MNSB) Friday said its board approved a new stock repurchase program of up to $10M, replacing and superseding the buyback plan announced in October 2025.
  • The timing, size and price of any buybacks will be at management’s discretion, with no guarantee of purchases, the company said.

data.title: WSFS Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.68 beats by $0.18, revenue of $275.3M beats by $7.09M
data.description:
• WSFS Financial press release (WSFS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.68 beats by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $275.3M (+7.5% Y/Y) beats by $7.09M.
  • At March 31, 2026, book value per share was $52.24, an increase of $0.97, or 2% (not annualized), from December 31, 2025.

data.title: Charter Communications' mixed Q1 results, broadband loss, weigh on shares
data.description:
Shares of Charter Communications (CHTR) are facing modest selling pressure on Friday morning following the release of mixed first-quarter results that included a decline in revenue and a larger-than-expected loss of broadband customers.

The parent company of Spectrum mobile and internet services added 368K new mobile lines (versus +432K estimates) during the quarter but lost 120K Spectrum Internet customers (versus -100K estimates) amid increased promotions from its competitors.

And although its video service lost 60K customers, it was less than the 85K decline analysts were expecting and compares to a loss of 181K customers in the same quarter last year. This can be attributed to Spectrum’s simplified pricing and packaging, along with the inclusion of programmers’ streaming applications in its expanded basic video packages.

For the first quarter, Charter Communications (CHTR) earned a profit of $9.17 per share in the first quarter, up 9% from a year ago but $0.91 below expectations. Adjusted EBITDA declined 2.2% to $5.6B, compressing adjusted EBITDA margin by 50 basis points to 41.5%.

On the revenue side, a 15.1% increase in mobile service revenue and advertising revenue was unable to fully compensate for losses in internet, video, voice, and residential service, all of which contributed to a 1.1% decline in revenue to a below-consensus $13.5B. 

The company’s balance sheet is also weighing on shares as free cash flow decreased 12.5% to $1.4B.

In response to the disappointing results, Charter Communications CEO Chris Winfrey assured investors that “as we continue to improve our products, pricing, packaging, and service, and complete our rural network initiatives, we are poised for improving customer and free cash flow growth.”

data.title: oint Stock Company Kaspi.kz raises $600M via oversubscribed 2031 bond issuance
data.description:
• Joint Stock Company Kaspi.kz (KSPI) issued $600M in 5.900% senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • The bond offering was 3.5x oversubscribed with demand from around 130 institutional investors.
  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes and to strengthen liquidity.
  • Coupon is set at 5.900%, with semi-annual payments starting October 28, 2026, and settlement on April 28, 2026.
